rootCause: The renderer pod mounts a ConfigMap named renderer-config as a volume, but no such ConfigMap exists in the cms namespace. The kubelet cannot populate the volume from an absent object, so it never creates the container: the pod stays in ContainerCreating with phase Pending and the kubelet repeatedly emits a FailedMount event naming the missing ConfigMap. The container image and command are healthy and would run a long-lived process if the volume could be mounted, so nothing about the workload itself is at fault. The fix is to create the renderer-config ConfigMap (or correct the volume reference), not to touch the container.
